category_title: Ambulant child with Autism and severe learning difficulties using AAC and behavioral protests
student_description: Adam is an 8-year-old who communicates through pre-symbolic behaviors, AAC, and physical actions, often protesting non-preferred transitions by sitting on the floor and spitting. The evidence reveals he uniquely requires adults to respect his need for physical space and a 10-minute calm-down period during dysregulation, as forcing proximity escalates his behavior. He also relies on concrete objects of reference to repair communication breakdowns and highly motivating sensory activities paired with non-verbal cues to successfully re-engage.

criteria.rationale: Adam needs alternative, tangible cues to process transitions and repair communication breakdowns when AAC symbols or verbal instructions fail.
criteria.check: The tutor's immediate response includes presenting a concrete physical object related to the activity to verify understanding or repair the communication breakdown.

criteria.rationale: Forcing proximity when Adam is protesting triggers intense spitting, so he requires physical space and a dedicated calm-down period to regulate.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly provides physical distance and a designated calm-down time (e.g., 10 minutes) in response to his spitting or dropping to the floor, avoiding forced proximity.

criteria.rationale: After dysregulation, Adam responds best to highly preferred sensory activities and non-verbal prompts rather than verbal demands.
criteria.check: The tutor attempts to re-engage Adam by introducing a highly motivating alternative activity (e.g., massage box) and using non-verbal cues (e.g., tapping the floor) to invite interaction.
